title: Die Wurst - The Official Dungeon Degenerates Zine - Quick Flip Through
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nF0VXXPcAh4

Daniel (@dungeondive) walks through the official *Die Wurst* zine that Sean Auburn and GoblinKo have just released as the first issue of their passion project. The content reads like a manifesto: it showcases the raw influences behind the game's grotesque beauty through Terry Gilliam film analysis, Ronnie James Dio's heavy metal legacy and influence on dark fantasy, favorite fantasy miniatures and tabletop inspirations, classic text adventure games like Zork and Hack that shaped procedural gameplay, and emerging artists like Leonard M whose skeleton artwork perfectly captures the outsider aesthetic. Each section explains why these influences shaped Dungeon Degenerates' visual and thematic language. The zine costs seven dollars, though shipping nearly matches the price—a reality check on supporting small indie publishers outside major distribution networks. Yet Daniel finds it worthwhile, celebrating how this supplemental material deepens connection to the game's lore and community. It's exactly the kind of passion project that builds cult followings around grungy, counter-cultural games.

----

What supplemental materials or zines have deepened your connection to a game's world? Would you be willing to pay for *Die Wurst* despite the shipping costs?
